name: SDLC Optional Assessor Blocking Pipeline
description: Software-delivery pipeline with one required assessor and one optional assessor.
outcome: Required assessments always gate progress; optional assessments only gate once instantiated.

definition.process_instructions:
Run the workflow through the authored stages `design-brief`, `implementation`, and `release-readiness`. Treat each stage as a distinct checkpoint of evidence and handoff quality: `design-brief` exists to The design is ready; `implementation` exists to The change is implemented and assessed; `release-readiness` exists to Release packaging is complete.
Use these roles deliberately: `block-design-architect` Produces the technical design for the feature; `block-delivery-engineer` Implements the planned change in the repository; `block-quality-assessor` Assesses implementation quality and regression risk; `block-security-assessor` Assesses security and misuse risk for the same subject; `block-release-manager` Packages the implementation after required assessments clear.
Happy path: start with the earliest unfinished stage, dispatch the best matching producing role for that stage, require a full handoff with concrete artifacts or evidence, then move to the next stage only when the current output is usable. When a later stage is primarily evaluative, dispatch the reviewer or assessor roles after the producing role has handed off; when a later stage is packaging or release-oriented, dispatch the coordinating role only after upstream evidence is complete.
Unhappy path: If any role reports missing evidence, partial delivery, request changes, rejection, blocking risk, or an escalation, keep the current subject open, route work back to the most relevant producing role, and require a fresh handoff before trying again. If two decisions conflict, preserve both in history, but route using the newest subject state and the most restrictive still-active finding. Do not skip a stage just because a later role believes it can improvise around incomplete upstream work. The optional assessor may still raise a blocking concern in this scenario. If that happens, keep the work open and route remediation, even though the assessor is not normally required for successor progress.
Completion rules: finish only after the last required stage has a final, internally consistent handoff, the final package matches the current subject state, and every role named above has either contributed or been intentionally skipped for a clear reason grounded in the playbook. Stage-specific closure for this playbook: each `design-brief` work item stays open until `block-design-architect` has either submitted current-subject handoffs that satisfy the `design-brief` goal or been intentionally skipped with a concrete reason grounded in the playbook; each `implementation` work item stays open until `block-delivery-engineer`, `block-quality-assessor`, and `block-security-assessor` have either submitted current-subject handoffs that satisfy the `implementation` goal or been intentionally skipped with a concrete reason grounded in the playbook; each `release-readiness` work item stays open until `block-release-manager` has either submitted current-subject handoffs that satisfy the `release-readiness` goal or been intentionally skipped with a concrete reason grounded in the playbook. Do not fabricate approvals, assessments, escalations, or branch changes that the playbook does not actually justify, and do not treat stale findings as permission to advance new work.

parameters.name: goal
parameters.description: What this live workflow should accomplish

parameters.name: change_request
parameters.description: Concrete repository change to implement in the seeded CLI workspace

parameters.name: optional_assessor_contract
parameters.description: When to skip or instantiate the optional assessor and what it must enforce

parameters.name: initial_revision_scope
parameters.description: What revision 1 must deliver before any required security rework is applied

parameters.name: rework_completion_scope
parameters.description: What revision 2 must add to satisfy the optional security assessor after request_changes

columns.label: Planned
columns.description: Work is defined but not yet assigned.

columns.label: Blocked
columns.description: Work is blocked and needs intervention.

columns.label: Done
columns.description: The work item meets its acceptance criteria.

stages.name: implementation
stages.goal: The change is implemented and assessed.
